page: Amazon Web Services c7g.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Graviton3 processor family, utilizing the arm64 architecture. It features 64 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, operating at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The processor is based on the ARMv8 Neoverse-V1 model, equipped with 64 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. The instance is provisioned with 128.0 GB of RAM, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of memory per core. It operates on the Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals strong multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores reaching strong and top-tier classifications respectively. Memory performance is highly efficient, as demonstrated by a top-tier PassMark memory score and strong uncached read and write memory bandwidth. The instance excels at software compilation, registering a top-tier score in Geekbench clang compilation. However, single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the average category. Additionally, while text generation for medium-sized LLM models is strong, text generation for smaller models is weak.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c7g.16xlarge offers high compute density and memory bandwidth relative to its resource profile, making it a viable option for multi-threaded workloads without the overhead of hyperthreading. The lack of local storage requires external storage attachments, which is a key architectural tradeoff. This instance is suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, static web serving, database operations, and medium-model LLM text generation.
description: Amazon Web Services c7g.16xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance powered by 64 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Graviton3 processor running at 2.6 GHz. It features 128.0 GB of RAM, a Nitro hypervisor, and 30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, with no local storage. The processor includes 32 MB of L3 cache and 1.0 MB of L2 cache per core. Benchmarks indicate top-tier multi-core performance and memory bandwidth, alongside strong software compilation and database operation capabilities. However, single-core performance remains average, and small-model LLM text generation is weak. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for highly parallelized workloads, offering high compute density without hyperthreading. It is suited for software compilation, static web serving, database operations, and multi-threaded processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7g.16xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance featuring 64 dedicated Graviton3 cores, 128.0 GB RAM, and 30 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core performance.

tagline: A dedicated 64-core arm64 compute instance delivering top-tier multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth for parallel workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated AWS Graviton3 physical cores running at 2.6 GHz
128.0 GB of RAM providing 2.0 GB of memory per core
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the Nitro hypervisor
No local storage, requiring network-attached storage solutions
Best suited for software compilation, database operations, and static web serving
